objective: minimize our CE fwd+bwd latency vs lifted-compiled baseline, s.t. peak <= compiled peak (keep memory saving) AND grad_rel < 1.5e-2
eval_opt_set: .autoresearch/ce_eval_local.py on RTX 3050, proxy N=4096 V=32000 H=512 (autonomous)
eval_held_out: python bench.py --compile ce_fit on Tesla T4 (N=16384 V=81000) — user runs

baseline.name: lifted compiled CE (ce_compiled.py): materialize (N,V) once, NO bwd recompute

best.change: fused online-softmax forward kernel (kill .float() (C,V) fp32 buffer; logsumexp+gather in 1 pass)
best.status: KEEP on local opt-set; AWAITING T4 ce_fit held-out confirmation

diagnosis: After iter1 we are GEMM-COUNT bound: ours backward = 3 GEMM (recompute-logits + grad_h + grad_w) vs compiled 2 GEMM (saved logits, no recompute); fwd both = 1 GEMM + fused reduce. So ours 4 GEMM vs compiled 3 -> ~1.33x floor, matches observed 1.37-1.44x. The extra GEMM IS the memory price (recompute instead of save). Going below 1.33x REQUIRES saving logits = spending compiled's memory = violates keep-memory.
open_levers: chunk-size/launch tuning (M2, expect small — already GEMM-bound)
grad-logit kernel block tiling (M2, expect small)
recompute=False fast mode = baseline path, for ce_fit where memory DOES fit (one auto-picking CE)
stop_reasoning: near recompute floor on low-mem path; further latency needs memory. Confirm iter1 on T4, then likely ship + add recompute=False fast-mode for the fits regime.
